localization.title: Help translate PaladinsCat
localization.description: Translations are maintained in the public locale repository and reviewed once on GitHub.
localization.signIn: A GitHub account is sufficient to contribute.
localization.signInOpen: Clone the locale repository, create a branch, and open a pull request.
localization.openDescription: GitHub stores translation files and provides the only review and merge gate.
localization.applyDescription: Choose a target language and translate only existing English keys.
localization.trustedDescription: Repository permissions and pull requests are managed on GitHub.
localization.denied: Update the branch and rerun validation before requesting review again.
localization.accessGranted: Repository access is managed by GitHub.
localization.noMySubmissions: You have not opened a translation pull request yet.
localization.published: Pull request created. Merge it after checks pass.
localization.localTolgeeDescription: Run Tolgee privately and use Tolgee CLI to push and pull files in your cloned locale repository.
localization.tokenOnce: Keep the Tolgee project API key on your computer and never commit it.
localization.error: Localization request failed. Please try again.
localization.noSubmissions: No submissions awaiting review or rebase.
localization.githubDescription: Clone the public locale repository, edit with Tolgee or a text editor, and open one GitHub pull request. PaladinsCat does not store translation drafts or require a second approval.
localization.workflowStepClone: Clone PaladinsCat-locales and create a translation branch.
localization.workflowStepTolgee: Import the English files into Tolgee, translate, and export the target files back into the clone.
localization.workflowStepPr: Validate, commit with GitHub Desktop, open a pull request, and merge after checks pass.
localization.deploymentDescription: Merged locale commits are pinned into the PaladinsCat frontend image for reproducible VPS deployments.
